channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says he respects Supreme Court's decision to deny his request to stop sentencing, vows to appeal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said he respects the Supreme Court’s decision to deny his request to stop his sentencing in New York v. Trump from moving forward, but said Thursday night he will appeal, while stressing that “lawfare" has been an “attack on the Republican Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court denies Trump attempt to stop sentencing in New York v. Trump
item.description: The United States Supreme Court has denied President-elect Trump's petition to block his Friday sentencing in New York v. Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrats join Republicans in voting to advance bill to detain migrants accused of crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Newly in the minority, Democrats voted with Republicans on Thursday to advance legislation that would require federal authorities to detain unauthorized immigrants who have been accused of certain crimes — signaling that they will try and find spots to work with President-elect Don
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic Sen. John Fetterman to meet with Trump at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Pennsylvania Sen. John Fetterman will become the chamber's first Democrat to meet with President-elect Donald Trump since the election, and plans to travel to Trump’s private resort in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 7 notable moments between VIPs at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former President Carter’s funeral service at Washington National Cathedral brought together all five living presidents and current and former officials for an event full of notable interactions. Carter died Dec. 29 at the age of 100 and was given a state funeral in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fani Willis seeks to overturn her disqualification from Trump Georgia election case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis has asked Georgia's highest court to review a lower appeals court's ruling that removed her from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A contentious push to wrap up the 9/11 prosecutions takes an emotional toll on victims' families
item.description: Sept. 11 survivors and victims’ relatives have mixed feelings about a planned plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump blames Newsom for California's raging wildfires
item.description: The wildfires that started on Tuesday have killed two people and destroyed more than 1,000 structures.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Senate approves committee assignments, tees up confirmation hearings next week
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday approved its organizing resolution for the 119th Congress, officially setting the composition of committees and greenlighting nomination hearings to take place in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Schools around the US are weighing responses to possible immigration raids
item.description: Schools around the country are reviewing what to do if immigration agents come knocking as President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office after campaigning on a pledge to deport millions of people.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-President Jimmy Carter's body arrives in Washington
item.description: The next phase of former President Jimmy Carter's state funeral has begun as his body is transported to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland, Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: “Look, the Panama Canal is vital to our country, it’s being operated by China, China. And we gave the Panama Canal to Panama, we didn’t give it to China,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin Republicans pushing to make voter ID a constitutional requirement
item.description: Other states have taken similar steps in recent years to put voter ID requirements in the constitution. Voters approved it in Mississippi in 2011 and North Carolina voters in 2018, while Minnesota voters rejected it in 2012.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: U.S. leaders honor former President Jimmy Carter legacy of service and citizenship
item.description: Carter, who died Dec. 29 at age 100, was transported to the Capitol by a horse-drawn cassion after arriving from Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota session agendas start to emerge as power fight drags on, acrimony picks up
item.description: A week ahead of the kick off of the 2025 legislative session, House leaders feuded over who is in charge and what might get accomplished amid a power struggle.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau resigns as Liberal Party leader, spelling end to time in power
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
